sections.paragraph:
ῥιπίζω aor. 3 sg. ἐρρίπισεν Da 2:35 (Aristoph. et al.) blow here and there, toss, of the wind (Da 2:35; EpArist 70), that sets a wave in motion on the water, pass. (Philo, Aet. M. 125 πρὸς ἀνέμων ῥιπίζεται τὸ ὕδωρ; a quot. in Dio Chrys. 15 [32], 23 δῆμος ἄστατον κακὸν καὶ θαλάσσῃ πανθʼ ὅμοιον ὑπʼ ἀνέμου ῥιπίζεται; Cass. Dio 70, 4 ῥιπιζομένη ἄχνη. See also Epict., Fgm. F 2 p. 487 Sch.) ὁ διακρινόμενος ἔοικεν κλύδωνι θαλάσσης ἀνεμιζομένῳ καὶ ῥιπιζομένῳ Js 1:6.—DELG s.v. ῥίπτω. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

quot. quot. = quotation

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 906
